calendar.matters:
Petition To Direct Executrix To Sell Real Property, Or Alternatively To Remove Executrix
Off Calendar Mar-11-2025 Advanced From Apr-21-25 To Mar-26-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Mar-11-2025 Advanced From Apr-21-25 To Mar-26-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Direct Executrix To Sell Real Property, Or Alternatively To Remove Executrix
Off Calendar Mar-26-2025 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Apr-01-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for Nancy Hill, Executor (via Zoom).
Christopher Land for Susan Hart and Dolores Tollestrup, Beneficiaries (via Zoom).
Susan Hart, Steve Hart, John Hart, and William Bello, in attendance (via Zoom).

The Court inquired whether the parties preferred to conclude the matter today, rather than continue the matter further. Both Mr. Land and Mr. Wrobel confirmed that all parties wished to conclude the matter.

Mr. Land requested a reduction of statutory fees under Probate Code Section 12205 due to delay and objected to supplemental fees for trust-related work. Mr. Wrobel responded that delays stemmed in part from the initial nominated executor declining to serve due to age, and that Ms. Hill, the successor executor, also had health challenges. He emphasized the complexity of the trust administration and asserted that Ms. Hill fulfilled her duties faithfully under difficult circumstances.

The Court raised concern about the proposed equal division of securities without itemization. Mr. Land indicated the beneficiaries had consulted UBS and did not anticipate issues.

The court took the petition for first and final account and distribution under submission with the intent to grant and reduce fees. The petition for preliminary distribution was taken off calendar as moot. The petition to remove the executrix is taken off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition To Direct Executrix To Sell Real Property, Or Alternatively To Remove Executrix
Off Calendar Jan-06-2025 Continued To Feb-10-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas S. Wrobel for Nancy Hill, Executor (via Zoom).
Christopher Land for Susan Hart and Dolores Tollestrup, Beneficiaries (via Zoom).
Nancy Hill was also present in the courtroom.

Mr. Wrobel reported that tax returns are nearing completion, a minor $600 accounting discrepancy remains unresolved, and a draft petition for final distribution has been shared with Mr. Land and is close to being finalized. Mr. Land expressed a desire to advance the preliminary distribution date from March 24, 2025, waiving formal notice if necessary.

The court continued the matter to February 10, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow additional time to finalize the petition for final distribution.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition To Direct Executrix To Sell Real Property, Or Alternatively To Remove Executrix
Off Calendar Dec-04-2024 Continued To Jan-06-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The First Account
Off Calendar Dec-04-2024 Continued To Jan-06-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-06-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas S. Wrobel for Nancy Hill, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Christopher Land for Susan Hart and Dolores Tollestrup (via Zoom).
Nancy Hill, Executor, present in court.
Susan Hart and Dolores Tollestrup, Beneficiaries, present via Zoom.

The court discussed recent filings and noted increased contention between the parties in this matter. Discussion was held as to whether the status report should be treated as a formal accounting. Mr. Wroble confirmed that his intent was to provide disclosure of information (bank records, receipts) in lieu of a formal Probate Code-compliant accounting.

Mr. Wrobel stated that receipts for expenses related to clearing the property before its sale would be provided. The court questioned whether the petition to remove the executor remains valid under current circumstances, as the property has been sold. Mr. Wrobel requested a 30-day extension to file the final petition for distribution, citing pending tax returns. Mr. Land expressed beneficiaries' frustrations with delays and lack of progress over two years, arguing for the removal of the executor. The court suggested to Mr. Wrobel to consider filing a petition for preliminary distribution if the final petition cannot be filed within 30 days.

The court took the report on the status of administration under submission with intent to grant and approved a 30-day extension for administration of the estate. The court set a hearing for status on close of estate for January 6, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and continued the 1) Petition to Direct the Sale or Remove the Executor and 2) Status of Filing of the First Account to be heard on the same date and time.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition To Direct Executrix To Sell Real Property, Or Alternatively To Remove Executrix
Off Calendar Nov-06-2024 Continued To Dec-04-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The First Account
Off Calendar Nov-06-2024 Continued To Dec-04-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared by zoom for Nancy Hill (executor).
Christopher Land appeared by zoom for Dolores Tollestrup and Susan Hart (beneficiaries).

Mr. Wrobel confirmed that the estate property was sold, closing on October 15, 2024. Mr. Land raised concerns regarding the delayed distribution to beneficiaries and expressed frustration with the progress of estate administration. Mr. Wrobel requested 60 days to finalize the estate tax returns and complete the remaining administrative tasks. Mr. Land argued for a shorter continuance to maintain accountability, noting prior delays in filings and reports. All matters are continued to Dec 4, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Santos. Not reported.

Mr. Wrobel confirmed that the estate property was sold, closing on October 15, 2024. REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-11-2024 Continued To Nov-06-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared by zoom for and with Nancy Hill (in person).
Christopher Land appeared by zoom for and with Dolores Tollestrup (beneficiary) by zoom and Susan Hart (beneficiary) by zoom.

Mrs. Hill stated that they received some offers, but she has not seen them. Mr. Wrobel stated that he filed an abbreviated accounting in the report, that he is ready to proceed with a final petition once the sale closes, that they received multiple offers over the asking price and requested that the petition to remove his client be taken off calendar. Mr. Land spoke of delay after delay, promised deadlines not having been met and asked that his petition not be taken off calendar. Further discussion was held regarding Mr. Land's petition having served a function in getting the administrator to act. Mr. Wrobel requested a 45 to 60-day continuance. The second report on status of administration, the Petition to Direct Executrix to Sell the Real Property, etc. and status on filing of the first account are continued to November 6, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

Petition To Direct Executrix To Sell Real Property, Or Alternatively To Remove Executrix
Off Calendar Sep-11-2024 Continued To Nov-06-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-05-2024 Continued To Sep-11-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared by zoom for and with Nancy Hill (in person).
Christopher Land appeared by zoom for and with Dolores Tollestrup and Susan hart (beneficiaries) by zoom.
Jonathan Kurniadi (real estate broker) appeared by zoom in propria persona.

Mr. Wrobel stated that the property is listed and that there have been well over one thousand inquiries. Mr. Land stated that the removal of Mrs. Hill continues to be his clients' position and request, that this was a last minute listing, that nothing was done in the last couple of years and that his clients have run out of patience with all of the delays. Further discussion was held regarding the appointment of a temporary administrator, whether this would create further delay, allowing additional time for the house to sell and the administrator proceeding with final distribution once the house is sold, Mr. Wrobel filing a petition for final distribution in 60-days if the house sells within the next 30-days, the estate being in this position for over a year, etc. The court noted that the mechanism used to request appointment of a special administrator was in the form of a status report, not a petition. Mr. Kurniadi stated that he wants to preserve any rights he has. The report on status of administration, the Petition to Direct Executrix to Sell the Real Property, etc. and status on filing of the first account are continued to September 11, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status of the real property being listed on the MSL is off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Pliego. Not reported.

Off Calendar Aug-05-2024 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Real Property Being Listed On The Msl
Off Calendar Jul-15-2024 Continued To Aug-05-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared by zoom for and with Nancy Hill (administrator) in person.
Christopher Land appeared by zoom for Susan Hart and Delores Tollestrup (beneficiaries).
Jonathan Kurniadi (real estate broker) in propria persona.

When asked, Mr. Kurniadi stated that the property is not listed for sale, that he has been trying to work with the estate representative, that there was a delay in getting the keys, that he went through the home and advised Mrs. Hill that he works with third parties to get the house in a condition to be listed, that he arranged for a terminate inspection and that Mrs. Hill wants to use her own workers. Mr. Kurniadi also stated that Mrs. Hill indicated that she feels pressured, spoke his experience in dealing with probate and trust properties, stated that he was just informed that Mrs. Hill has engaged another broker, that his listing agreement was signed on June 10th for 90-days terminating on September 8th and that he has a contract that is enforceable. Mr. Land stated that Mr. Kurniadi's declaration confirms what his clients have been saying for the last two years and that Mrs. Hill has reluctantly given up control. Further discussion was held regarding one of the beneficiaries being 95 years of age and still waiting to receive her money, Mrs. Hill's feeling that the court forced her to enter into the listing agreement, Mrs. Hill no longer wanting to work with Mr. Kurniadi, Mrs. Hill's promise/position that she will have the house listed by August 30th, Mrs. Hill being told by the other agent that the listing agreement with Mr. Kurniadi was not a legal agreement, the family history, Mr. Kand's clients expecting to pursue removal and replacement of Mrs. Hill at the August 5th hearing, Mr. Land's concern about the estate being involved in breach of contract, Mrs. Hill's administration of the trust, etc. Status on listing of the real property on the MSL is continued to August 5, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court informed Mrs. Hill that she was close to being removed today and suggested that Mr. Land have an alternate in mind if he wants Mrs. Hill removed or suspended.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Tan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Bond
Off Calendar Jun-24-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared by zoom for administrator.
Chris Land appeared by zoom for and with Susan Hart by zoom and for Dolores Tollestrup (beneficiaries).

Mr. Wrobel stated that Mrs. Hill received the bond and that his assistant filed it this morning. The clerk confirmed that the bond has been filed. Mr. Land raised the issue of the real property needing to be listed within two weeks of the date of the last hearing and stated that it is not listed yet. Mr. Wrobel stated that he will look into it. Status on filing of the bond is off calendar. Status on the real property being listed on the MSL is set for July 15, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If proof of the listing is filed prior to the hearing, the status hearing will go off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Pliego. Not reported.

Petition To Direct Executrix To Sell Real Property, Or Alternatively To Remove Executrix

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-10-2024 Continued To Aug-05-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared by zoom for and with Nancy Hill (petitioner/executor) in person.
Chris Land appeared by zoom for and with Susan Hart by zoom and for Delores Tollestrup (beneficiaries).
Jonathan Kurniadi (real estate broker) in propria persona.
Tom Harris (real estate agent from Century 21 Baldini) in propria persona.

Discussion was held regarding the issues raised in the tentative ruling with respect to Mrs. Hill's petition. Mr. Kurniadi stated that he has a listing agreement, but it has not been executed. Mr. Harris requested that he be allowed to co-list the property. Mr. Wrobel objects. The matter was passed to give Mr. Kurniadi and Mrs. Hill the opportunity to go over Mrs. Hill's questions and to sign the listing agreement. The matter was re-called. Mr. Kurniadi stated that he has the executed 90-day listing agreement and that he knows his job is to expedite the sale. Discussion was held regarding the possibility of a 14-day listing. Mr. Wrobel requested a continuance and stated that he will address the examiner's notes. The Petition to Direct Executrix to Sell Real Property, etc., and the Second Report on Status of Estate Administration, etc. are continued to August 5, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing the first account is also set for August 5, 2024. The accounting is to be filed by July 29, 2024. Status on filing of bond in the amount of $2,500,000.00 is set for June 24, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court noted that there has been undue delay in administration of the estate.

Judge: Cecilia Castellanos. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Malone/Santos. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-21-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel, via zoom, for Nancy Hill, in court, Petitioner.

The court confirms with Mr. Wrobel that Joan Bello had a trust naming William Bello and Susan Hart as sole beneficiaries. The court also confirms the spelling of the decedent's last name as Blecher. After additional discussion, the court asks if there are any appearances to object to this petition. None are presented. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated Jul-26-2017 is admitted to probate. Nancy Hill is appointed Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Karen Ann Simons is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Apr-03-2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed by Mar-21-2023 the status hearing will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Teebay. Not reported.
